metadata.archetype_justification: The project implements a FHIR JPA server with full CRUD operations on healthcare resources (Patient, Encounter, Observation, etc.), uses JPA/Hibernate persistence with multiple database backends (PostgreSQL, Oracle, MSSQL, MySQL), and manages entity lifecycle through a two-tier DAO pattern. Classified as stateful-crud.
metadata.context: Open-source Java implementation of the HL7 FHIR healthcare standard.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute infrastructure defined — no ECS/EKS/Lambda/Fargate
top_gaps.impact: Cannot deploy to cloud-native compute without full containerization effort

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No VPC, security groups, or network segmentation defined
top_gaps.impact: No network isolation for deployed workloads

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code
top_gaps.gap: Zero IaC coverage — all infrastructure would be manual
top_gaps.impact: No reproducible deployments, no disaster recovery automation

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or audit logging infrastructure defined
top_gaps.impact: No compliance audit trail for healthcare data

top_gaps.title: Centralized Identity
top_gaps.impact: No SSO or federated identity for deployed instances

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists — extensive hapi-fhir-docs module, README.md, CLAUDE.md, changelog YAML files
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that indexes HAPI FHIR documentation and FHIR specification details to answer developer questions about configuration, deployment, and FHIR resource handling
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index the hapi-fhir-docs module content into a vector store; configure Amazon Bedrock for RAG

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 2) — GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps pipelines configured with build and test stages
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers builds, monitors test results across the 256-parallel module test matrix, identifies flaky tests, and reports build status
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose GitHub Actions API and Azure DevOps API as agent tools; configure service account permissions

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Structured tracing in place (OPS-Q1 = 3) — OpenTelemetry 2.10.0 instrumentation integrated
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that queries traces, correlates performance issues across FHIR operations, and suggests optimization targets
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Deploy OpenTelemetry collector with queryable backend (X-Ray); expose trace query API as agent tool

findings.title: No managed compute infrastructure defined
findings.description: No managed compute infrastructure is defined. The repository contains no Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, ECS task definitions, EKS configurations, or Lambda function definitions. The only Dockerfile is a CI build image, not a runtime container.
findings.gap: All compute infrastructure is undefined — no managed container orchestration or serverless compute. Deployment model is entirely manual.
findings.recommendation: Define EKS cluster infrastructure in CDK/Terraform. Create a production Dockerfile for the Spring Boot application. Deploy on EKS with Fargate node pools.

findings.title: No managed database infrastructure defined
findings.description: No managed database infrastructure is defined. The application supports PostgreSQL, Oracle, MSSQL, MySQL/MariaDB, and H2 via JDBC drivers but no IaC provisions any database instance.
findings.gap: All databases would be self-managed or manually provisioned. No RDS, Aurora, or DynamoDB resources defined.
findings.recommendation: Provision Aurora PostgreSQL via IaC with Multi-AZ, automated backups, and auto-scaling. Use DynamoDB for session/cache workloads.

findings.title: Custom workflow orchestration without managed service backing
findings.description: The hapi-fhir-storage-batch2 module implements a custom workflow orchestration framework with job definitions, work chunk processing, and reduction steps. This is entirely bespoke — not Step Functions, Temporal, or any managed service.
findings.gap: Workflow orchestration exists but is entirely custom with no managed service backing. Requires operational maintenance and lacks visibility of managed orchestration.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate migrating Batch2 coordination to AWS Step Functions or maintain the custom framework on managed compute (EKS) with proper monitoring.

findings.title: In-process messaging without managed external infrastructure
findings.description: The application uses Spring's internal messaging framework for FHIR Subscription delivery and Batch2 work distribution. No external managed messaging infrastructure (SQS, SNS, EventBridge, MSK) exists.
findings.gap: Messaging is entirely in-process. For cross-service state changes, managed external messaging should provide durability and decoupling.
findings.recommendation: Adopt EventBridge for FHIR resource change events and SQS for Batch2 work chunk queuing.

findings.title: No network security infrastructure defined
findings.description: No VPC, subnets, security groups, NACLs, or network segmentation configuration exists in the repository.
findings.gap: Network isolation is entirely undefined. A healthcare application handling PHI/PII requires strict network segmentation.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC with private subnets for EKS and Aurora. Use security groups with least-privilege rules. Deploy VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

findings.title: No API entry point defined
findings.description: No API gateway, load balancer, or CloudFront distribution is defined. The application uses embedded Jetty 12 as its HTTP server with no managed entry point infrastructure.
findings.gap: Direct service exposure with no throttling, authentication gateway, or request validation at the edge.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway in front of the FHIR server for throttling, authentication, request validation, and usage tracking.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configured
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists. No ASG, ECS service scaling, EKS HPA, or Lambda concurrency settings are defined.
findings.gap: All capacity would be statically provisioned. A FHIR server handling variable clinical workloads needs elastic scaling.
findings.recommendation: Configure Kubernetes HPA on EKS based on request rate and response latency. Configure Aurora auto-scaling for read replicas.

findings.title: No backup and recovery configuration
findings.description: No backup or recovery configuration exists. No automated backup plans, retention periods, or PITR settings are defined.
findings.gap: Healthcare data requires robust backup and recovery. No backup infrastructure means potential permanent data loss.
findings.recommendation: Configure Aurora automated backups with 35-day retention and PITR. Create AWS Backup plans with cross-region replication.

findings.title: No high availability or fault isolation
findings.description: No multi-AZ deployment configuration exists. No availability zone configuration, cross-zone load balancing, or fault isolation patterns are defined.
findings.gap: Single point of failure risk. Healthcare systems require HA for patient care continuity.
findings.recommendation: Deploy EKS across 3 AZs. Configure Aurora Multi-AZ with automatic failover.

findings.title: Zero Infrastructure as Code coverage
findings.description: No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm charts, or Kustomize files exist. All infrastructure would need to be created manually.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. No reproducible deployments, no environment consistency, no disaster recovery automation.
findings.recommendation: Adopt CDK or Terraform to define all infrastructure: EKS cluster, Aurora PostgreSQL, VPC/networking, API Gateway, monitoring, and backup plans.

findings.title: CI/CD automation limited to build/test — no deployment
findings.description: Dual CI/CD pipelines exist (GitHub Actions + Azure DevOps) with automated build and test stages. However, there is no automated deployment stage — releases publish library artifacts to Maven Central but do not deploy services.
findings.gap: Build is automated but deployment is manual. No automated infrastructure or service deployment pipeline exists.
findings.recommendation: Extend CI/CD with deployment stages: container image build → push to ECR → deploy to EKS via ArgoCD/Flux (GitOps).

findings.title: Monolith with identifiable modules but shared database schemas
findings.description: 60+ Maven modules compile into a single deployable unit. Modules have clear responsibilities with CODEOWNERS but share a database schema and have cross-module data access through shared DAO layers.
findings.gap: Shared database schemas and cross-module entity access prevent independent deployment. ResourceTable and related entities are accessed from subscription, MDM, batch, and search modules.
findings.recommendation: Begin Strangler Fig decomposition starting with the Subscription service. Define service contracts between modules before extraction.

findings.title: No standard API versioning strategy
findings.description: No traditional REST API versioning is used. The application versions by FHIR specification version determined at deployment time, not URL path or header-based versioning.
findings.gap: No explicit versioning mechanism for custom operations and non-FHIR endpoints. Breaking changes cannot be versioned independently of FHIR specification version.
findings.recommendation: Implement API versioning for custom operations and admin endpoints. Consider header-based versioning to maintain FHIR specification compliance.

findings.title: No CloudTrail or audit logging infrastructure
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging infrastructure is defined. Hibernate Envers provides data-level auditing but no infrastructure-level audit logging exists.
findings.gap: No infrastructure audit logging. For healthcare applications, CloudTrail with immutable logs is a compliance requirement (HIPAA, HITRUST).
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock. Configure CloudWatch Logs for application audit events.

findings.title: Authentication available in code but not enforced at infrastructure level
findings.description: The application provides AuthorizationInterceptor and ConsentInterceptor and supports OIDC. However, no API Gateway authorizer or Cognito user pool is configured at the infrastructure level.
findings.gap: Authentication must be manually configured by deployers. No managed authorizer enforcement at the edge.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway with Cognito or OIDC authorizer. Enforce authentication at the gateway for all external endpoints.

findings.title: No centralized identity provider integration
findings.description: No centralized IdP integration is defined at the infrastructure level. OIDC support exists in code but no Cognito, Okta, or SAML federation is configured.
findings.gap: No centralized IdP. Healthcare applications require federated identity for clinical user access.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito. Configure OIDC federation with clinical identity providers. Enable SSO.

findings.title: No alerting or anomaly detection configured
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration, no error rate thresholds, no latency alarms defined.
findings.gap: No alerting of any kind. Performance degradation or errors would go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch alarms on FHIR operation latency (p99), error rates, and connection pool utilization. Add anomaly detection.

findings.title: No deployment strategy exists
findings.description: No service deployment strategy. CI/CD pipelines publish library artifacts to Maven Central — they do not deploy services. No blue/green, canary, or rolling deployment.
findings.gap: Any service deployment would be manual with no staged rollout, no automatic rollback, no traffic shifting.
findings.recommendation: Implement canary deployments on EKS using Argo Rollouts or Flagger with automatic rollback on elevated error rates.

findings.title: No incident response automation or runbooks
findings.description: No incident response automation or runbooks exist. No SSM documents, no Lambda remediation, no structured runbooks.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc. No documented procedures for common FHIR server incidents.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common incidents and automate remediation via Systems Manager Automation.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No resource tagging configuration exists. No default_tags, no tagging standards, no Tag Policies or Config rules.
findings.gap: Zero tagging governance. Cost allocation, ownership, and environment identification undefined.
findings.recommendation: Define a tagging standard (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter, DataClassification) and enforce via CDK/Terraform default_tags.

evaluations.reason: archetype-N/A: no deployed workload with user-facing surface for SLOs

triggering_questions.note: Monolith with identifiable modules but shared DB schemas

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 AND no deployment container definitions found

triggering_questions.note: No managed compute; only CI build Dockerfile exists

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures); application logic is database-portable across open-source engines (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB)

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures; all logic in application layer — no commercial DB lock-in

pathways.not_triggered_reason: DATA-Q4=4 — no stored procedures or proprietary SQL. While Oracle and MSSQL drivers are present, the application is fully portable to open-source engines without schema conversion.

triggering_questions.note: No managed database infrastructure — all databases would be self-managed or manual

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads detected; no streaming/ETL artifacts found
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found during discovery. The application has no data processing responsibilities beyond FHIR resource storage and retrieval.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=1 (zero IaC) AND INF-Q11=2 (no deployment automation) AND OPS-Q5=1 (no deployment strategy)

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context describes 'Open-source Java implementation of the HL7 FHIR healthcare standard' which does not contain AI-related signal terms.
